scope: Narrowed re-check of specific points after round-1 fixes. Only targeted claims re-verified; clean areas not re-audited.

findings.issue: Task 6 uses `Locale.ROOT` for `SimpleDateFormat("yyyyMMdd-HHmm", Locale.ROOT)` while Task 7 uses `Locale.US` for the identical format string and purpose. Both locales produce ASCII-only digits and are safe. However, the inconsistency means a developer following Task 6 and a developer following Task 7 will write slightly different code for the same filename pattern. Round-1 fix correctly aligned the API (SimpleDateFormat vs java.time) but left the Locale divergence.
findings.fix: Pick one locale and apply it to both tasks. `Locale.US` is slightly more explicit for ASCII-digit filenames and is what Task 7 already specifies. Change Task 6 line 35 and the imports hint (line 43, already has `java.util.Locale`) to use `Locale.US` to match Task 7.

task_6.nanoTime_lambda_syntax: PASS — Task 6 line 139 now correctly reads `nowNanos = { System.nanoTime() }` with explicit note that `::System.nanoTime` bound reference does not compile in Kotlin. Round-1 fix applied correctly.
task_6.SimpleDateFormat_format_matches_task4: PASS — Task 6 line 35 uses `SimpleDateFormat("yyyyMMdd-HHmm", Locale.ROOT).format(Date())`. Format string `yyyyMMdd-HHmm` matches Task 4 pattern. API is consistent (java.text.SimpleDateFormat, not java.time). Minor Locale divergence from Task 7 noted as finding above.

Task 1=strings, Task 3=LogExportManager+TapCounter, Task 4=CrashReportActivity UI (needed for end-to-end dev-gesture user-verify), Task 5=CrashHandler installed + manifest entry. All four dependencies are substantively justified in the task description (lines 26, 119-131). No hallucinated deps detected.
